messages.content:
When descending in a multirotor, which of the following is correct?

Review Later
You may descend as quick as you like
Descending and flying sideways or forward will eliminate the risk of vortex ring state
Descending vertically is safer than descending diagonally
With a headwind, glide distance will increase.
Descending vertically is safer than descending diagonally.

The cross section of a propeller resembles what?

A wing cross section
A circle
A flat surface
A wheel

Where can Prohibited, Restricted and Danger Areas be found?

VTC
VNC
Google
WAC Chart
Prohibited, Restricted, and Danger Areas can be found on a WAC Chart.

What information is contained in the ERSA?

Weather and Terrain
Licensed and registered aerodromes, radio frequencies, radio navigation aids, runway and taxiway information
Prohibited, restricted and danger areas, including emergency procedures
Licensed and registered aerodromes, radio frequencies, radio navigation aids, runway and taxiway information and prohibited, restricted and danger areas, including emergency procedures

The correct answer is: Licensed and registered aerodromes, radio frequencies, radio navigation aids, runway and taxiway information, and prohibited, restricted, and danger areas, including emergency procedures.

Where should you find information about Prohibited, restricted and dangerous areas.

AIP
NOTAM
ERSA
Microsoft Flight Sim
Information about prohibited, restricted, and dangerous areas can be found in the Aeronautical Information Publication (AIP).

What APP is available that covers all electronic charts, AIP and ERSA in the one location

Open Sky
OK2Fly
Mission Planner
Electronic Flight bag like OzRunways or AV Plan
An electronic flight bag like OzRunways or AV Plan is available that covers all electronic charts, AIP, and ERSA in one location.

how do we identify the location of a non-controlled aerodrome

ERSA
Google maps
Bing maps
DJI Go

To identify the location of a non-controlled aerodrome, you can refer to the ERSA (En Route Supplement Australia) publication. ERSA provides information about licensed and registered aerodromes, including their locations and other relevant details.

RPA operational requirements outside controlled airspace

may be conducted below 400 ft above ground level (AGL) near noncontrolled aerodromes, but not over the movement area or the vicinity of the approach and departure paths
may be conducted below 400 ft above mean sea level (AMSL) near noncontrolled aerodromes, but not over the movement area or the vicinity of the approach and departure paths
may be conducted below 400 ft above mean sea level (AMSL) anywhere near and over non controlled aerodromes
may be conducted below 400 ft above ground level (AGL) anywhere near and over non controlled aerodromes

The correct answer is: RPA operational requirements outside controlled airspace may be conducted below 400 ft above ground level (AGL) near non-controlled aerodromes, but not over the movement area or the vicinity of the approach and departure paths.

What is the function of IMU

To provide data to the flight controller
To provide height information
To transmit live feed video
To measure obstacle distance

The function of an IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit) is to provide data to the flight controller. It consists of sensors such as accelerometers and gyroscopes that measure and provide information about the aircraft's orientation, velocity, acceleration, and other motion-related parameters. This data is then used by the flight controller to stabilize the aircraft and control its movements.
